exemplar.title: Will the AI-assisted creation of Coca-Cola Y3000 enhance the company's profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: Will the AI-assisted creation of Coca-Cola Y3000 enhance the company's profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Correctly identifies âCreativityâ as the key concept and distinguishes its adaptive and innovative forms
Consistently links creative problem-solving to AI-generated flavour development and engagement with Gen-Z consumers
Anchors discussion in Coca-Cola Y3000âs data (impressions, margins, training requirements) to show organisational relevance
weaknesses: Integration is uneven, with sections merely tagging âcreativeâ onto descriptions without deeper reflection
Lacks academic definitions or theoretical frameworks (e.g., Amabile) to deepen conceptual analysis
Some generic statements about AI trends dilute the conceptual focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the IA shows good understanding of creativity and strong organisational links, integration is not fully sustained and lacks deeper theoretical underpinning. This aligns with a band-3 assessment for Integration of a Key Concept.

strengths: Includes exactly five supporting documents, meeting the quantity requirement
Bibliography is clearly labeled and complete, demonstrating attention to required elements
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent with HTML artifacts and missing access dates
Evidence is mostly secondary and lacks in-depth primary data, with some source repetition
Diagram captions and footnotes are mis-formatted or duplicated without clear data sourcing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the IA provides a sufficient number of relevant documents, inconsistent formatting and limited depth of primary evidence position this work at a band-2 for Supporting Documents.

strengths: Selects three established business tools (Ansoff, PLC, margin analysis) relevant to the research question
Applies each tool to Y3000, linking analyses to profitability outcomes
weaknesses: No rationale provided for why these specific tools best address the research question
Explanations sometimes remain descriptive and assert causality rather than proving it
Missed opportunities for comparative benchmarking to deepen insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and application are adequate and generally relevant, but lack consistently clear and effective justification, corresponding with a band-3 performance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Incorporates quantitative data (revenue growth, margin tables, media impressions) accurately
Evaluates benefits versus risks (brand relevance vs. training costs, cyber threats) across tools
weaknesses: Data selection is limited to a single metric set without triangulation
Links between metrics and financial outcomes are sometimes superficial or unsupported
Key analytical gaps (e.g., missing ROI estimates or external factor discussion) weaken depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA demonstrates adequate analysis and reasonable evaluation of arguments, but data use is limited and analysis not consistently deep, meriting a band-3 for Analysis and Evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions directly answer the research question and are fully consistent with the evidence presented
Summarises key findings on profitability improvements from AI-assisted Y3000 launch
weaknesses: Synthesis is brief and does not distinguish which framework most strongly drives profitability
Lacks nuanced evaluation of alternative scenarios or limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion explicitly addresses the research question and aligns with evidence, satisfying all descriptors for a band-3 performance.

strengths: Follows IB conventions with title page, contents, numbered sections
Logical progression from introduction through analysis to conclusion
weaknesses: Minor lapses in paragraphing and duplicated footnotes
Table of contents lacks page numbers, reducing navigability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is appropriate and coherent throughout, meeting the criteria for a band-2 score.

strengths: All required elements (title, RQ, word count, bibliography, appendices) are present
Consistent formatting of text and headings supports readability
weaknesses: Formatting errors in tables and figures (missing units, incomplete captions)
Table of contents formatting lacks dot-leaders, and some sentences end abruptly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor formatting and clarity issues, all compulsory elements are included and presentation supports the IAâs readability, corresponding to a band-2 performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows IB conventionsâtitle page, table of contents, numbered sectionsâand ideas flow logically from introduction to conclusion.  Minor lapses in paragraphing and some duplicated footnotes do not undermine overall coherence.

Sources include quarterly financial statements, corporate press releases, marketing/consumer-behaviour articles and award coverage.  They are all relevant to profitability and AI but depth is mixed; most evidence is secondary commentary rather than primary internal data, and there is some repetition of the same article.  Range and depth are therefore âsufficientâ rather than âin-depth and wide-ranging.â
All compulsory elements (title, RQ, key concept, word count, bibliography, sources list, appendices/figures) are present.  Formatting errors exist but nothing essential is missing.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed and referenced, meeting the 3-5 requirement.  Citation style is consistent enough to be traceable, fulfilling the expectations for this strand.

Each tool is applied to Y3000 and linked to profitability (e.g., PLC phases tied to sales data; Ansoff quadrant risk; margin trends pre/post launch).  Yet explanations are occasionally descriptive and causality is asserted rather than proven, so relevance is adequate but not consistently âclear and effective.â

The IA incorporates basic quantitative data: revenue growth %, margin table, media-impressions figure.  Data are accurate and used in discussion, satisfying adequacy, but selection is limited (only one internal metric set) and not triangulated, preventing it from being classified as âsufficientâ or âeffective.â

Analysis goes beyond description by weighing benefits (brand relevance, cost efficiencies) against risks (training costs, cyber threats) and links these to profitability across the three tools.  Integration between data and argument is present but occasionally superficial; still, overall analysis is mostly effective with reasonable coherence.

The research questionâwhether AI-assisted Y3000 will enhance profitabilityâis directly addressed in the final paragraph with a clear affirmative stance supported by summarised evidence, fulfilling the descriptor for an explicit answer.
Three standard BM tools (Ansoff, PLC, margin analysis) are selected.  This constitutes an adequate, though not especially broad or innovative, toolbox for answering the RQ.

The conclusion explicitly answers the research question and is consistent with evidence cited in each section (acknowledges fluctuations yet overall profit improvement).  Although synthesis is brief and lacks nuanced evaluation of alternative outcomes, it remains aligned with presented findings.

Throughout the IA, the student anchors discussion firmly in Coca-Cola Y3000âintroducing corporate data, marketing activities, cost implications and consumer reactions.  Analysis is consistently tied back to the organisation (e.g., press-generated 5.2 billion impressions, specific quarterly margins, training requirements for AI staff).  Some paragraphs drift into generic statements about AI trends, indicating partial rather than total integration; nonetheless the connection is strong and sustained.

The student defines the key concept of creativity, distinguishes adaptive from innovative creativity, and repeatedly links AI-generated flavour development to the notion of âcreativeâ problem-solving.  References to how creativity helps Coca-Cola remain relevant (e.g., engaging Gen-Z consumers, unique QR experience) show clear conceptual understanding and analytical application across all three tools.  However, integration is unevenâseveral sections merely tag the word âcreativeâ onto descriptive points without deep conceptual reflectionâso full, seamless integration is not yet evident.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated but could be refined by specifying the timeframe and performance metrics to be evaluated (e.g., profitability ratios over specific quarters) for greater precision.
The student correctly identifies âCreativityâ as the key concept but should integrate academic definitions (e.g., Amabileâs creativity framework) to deepen theoretical understanding.
The table of contents follows a logical order but omits page numbers, which reduces navigability; include accurate page references for each section.
Some TOC entries are left-aligned without leaders or dots, making it hard to match headings to page numbers visually; apply consistent formatting with dot-leaders.
The background explains AIâs impact but does not link specific industry data to profitability; tie the AI trend statement explicitly to the research question.
The sentence ends abruptly at âincredible potential of AI inâ; ensure complete sentences to maintain clarity and coherence throughout the introduction.
Footnotes display HTML artifacts (e.g., âlt;sup>â); standardize citation formatting to improve readability and traceability.
You list three business tools but do not justify why these specific frameworks best address the research question; provide rationale for their selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The explanation of the product-development quadrant is descriptive but lacks risk analysis (e.g., financial, operational); deepen the link to profitability metrics.
Footnote citations on this page are inconsistent (varying styles and missing Accessed dates); ensure uniform reference style across all sources.
You describe media impressions but do not quantify their impact on revenue or margins; explicitly connect PR metrics to financial outcomes.
There is a duplicated and mis-formatted footnote here using math tags; remove non-relevant LaTeX elements and consolidate duplicates.
The Ansoff discussion notes moderate risk but does not quantify investment levels or ROI; include numerical estimates to strengthen evaluation.
Consider benchmarking Coca-Colaâs Y3000 lifecycle against competitor product launches to provide industry context and comparative insight.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Diagram caption lacks note on data source and date range; include clear labeling to validate the product life-cycle chart.
When describing PLC stages, explicitly link each phase (e.g., introduction, maturity) to profit-margin movements to enhance analytical depth.
Avoid generic qualifiers like âeffective tacticsâ; use precise terms (e.g., â15% reduction in churnâ) to quantify creative impact.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The decline stage description notes a 9.24% drop but omits factors such as competitor promotions; discuss external influences in your analysis.
Table columns lack unit labels for revenue and profit; add â(million US$)â in headers to clarify the scale of figures.
âTable 1: Coca-Colaâs profit margin from Q4 2022 â Q3 2023â is inconsistent with the inclusion of Q1 2024 data; revise caption to match the data range.
The chart caption for Figure 4 omits explanation of axis labels and data points; include legend and source to improve interpretability.
The assertion that GPM declines due to a 6% cost increase is unsupported by cost-of-goods data; include COGS figures to validate this claim.
The net profit analysis jumps to NPM without summarizing GPM trends; provide a brief synthesis of margin changes to guide the reader.
The concluding paragraph restates findings but lacks synthesis of which factor (Ansoff, PLC, or margins) most strongly drives profitability; integrate findings for a more nuanced conclusion.
The bibliography section is clearly labeled and complete, demonstrating careful attention to required elements.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent capitalization and punctuation; adopt a single referencing style (e.g., APA) and apply it uniformly.
